summary: Full regression testing of 4 critical fixes completed. All backend tests passed (11/12, 1 skipped due to auth). Frontend collections page verified working correctly with tier pricing display, sale badges, and color swatches.

success_rate.frontend: 100% - Collections page and sale page verified

context_for_next_testing_agent: All 4 fixes verified as working correctly. See detailed verification below.

details: GET /api/tiles/collections returns tier_pricing_disabled flag for each collection
When tier_pricing_disabled=true, prices_from equals min_price (no tier discount applied)
When tier_pricing_disabled=false, prices_from is less than min_price (tier discount applied)
Frontend TileCollectionsPage.js correctly displays min_price for tier-disabled collections
Code verified at tiles.py lines 678-686: tier_pricing_disabled products add base price to best_prices, others add discounted price
PUT /api/supplier-sync/products/sale-pricing with was_markup_percent=25 stores discount_percentage=25 exactly
Tested with 25%, 15%, 30% - all stored exactly as entered
When sale_active=false, was_price, discount_percentage, sale_savings are all cleared to null
Code verified at supplier_sync.py lines 5458-5459: when was_markup_percent is provided, discount_percentage is set to that exact value
Migration sync_discount_percentage_v1 syncs existing products (server.py lines 8574-8601)
GET /api/specifications/types/by-group returns groups with nested specifications arrays
Response structure: [{name: 'Physical Properties', specifications: [{name: 'Material'}, {name: 'Edge'}, ...]}, ...]
BulkCategoryEditorSections.jsx correctly parses nested structure (lines 248-259)
Groups: Physical Properties (4 specs), Appearance (2 specs), Safety (1 spec), Usage (1 spec)
Spec pre-population uses spec_${field} without underscore-to-dash conversion (SupplierProducts.js line 3580)
Publish endpoint uses sp.get('product_group') from supplier_products first (supplier_sync.py line 2576)
Falls back to product_group parameter, then defaults to 'tiles' only if neither is set
Migration fix_product_group_routing_v1 syncs product_group from supplier_products to tiles (server.py lines 8603-8650)
Collections can be filtered by group parameter: /api/tiles/collections?group=materials
sale_active=false correctly clears was_price, discount_percentage, sale_savings to null
Tested via API: all fields cleared when sale disabled
tile_doc includes: tier_pricing_disabled, has_custom_tier_pricing, tier_discounts, sale_active, was_price, discount_percentage
Code verified at supplier_sync.py lines 2578-2589
Standard tiles (tier_pricing_disabled=false) have prices_from < min_price
Default tier discounts [5,10,15,20] applied correctly (20% max discount)

api_tests_performed.GET /api/specifications/types/by-group: Returns 5 groups with nested specifications arrays

frontend_tests_performed.collection_cards: Display prices, color swatches, labels (CLEARANCE, BEST SELLER)

code_review_verification.BulkCategoryEditorSections.jsx: Lines 248-259 correctly parse nested spec structure
code_review_verification.SupplierProducts.js: Line 3580 uses spec_${field} without dash conversion
